content.problemStatement:
You are given two binary strings SSS and TTT, each of length NNN. You wish to convert SSS into TTT using the following operation any number of times (possibly zero):


Choose two distinct indices iii and jjj (1≤i,j≤N1 ≤ i, j ≤ N1≤i,j≤N, i≠ji ≠ ji=j) such that both Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 and Sj=1S_j = 1Sj​=1, and change both to Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0 and Sj=0S_j = 0Sj​=0.
Determine whether it is possible to transform SSS into TTT by applying the above operation.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the length of the string.
The second line contains SSS - the initial string.
The third line contains TTT - the target string.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing `Yes` if SSS can be converted to TTT, or `No` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1 : We can choose i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2 and convert Si=Sj=0S_i = S_j = 0Si​=Sj​=0 to get S=00S = 00S=00.
Test case 2 : It is impossible to go from SSS to TTT.
